relevant_files.summary: Codex client detection and capability matrix live here; current matrix marks Codex hook/skill unsupported.
Codex config writer updates ~/.codex/config.toml with MCP server blocks only.
Init installs Claude-specific skill/hook/settings assets but no Codex equivalents.
Tests codify that Codex currently shows manual follow-up rather than installed skill/hook support.
Initialization docs are Claude-centric and describe non-Claude clients as lacking automated handoff.
Previous analysis already identified Codex as partial support and suggested capability matrix improvements.

patterns.description: Capabilities are statically expressed per client and consumed by init summary output.
Init currently writes client follow-up assets only for Claude.
Codex diverges from JSON-based clients only at MCP config layer.
Current UX expectation intentionally treats Codex as partially integrated.

constraints: There are existing uncommitted changes in templates/bootstrap/* and templates/claude-skills/*; implementation work must avoid overwriting them without coordination.
Fabric init is intentionally non-destructive and preserves existing files/settings unless forced.
Any Codex integration should preserve current TOML MCP config behavior and add assets incrementally.

integration_points.description: Update Codex capabilities and possibly expose finer-grained capability states.
Install repo-scoped Codex assets and adjust reason/follow-up output.
Potentially add support for skill toggles or Codex feature guidance in config.toml.
Document Codex parity path and any hook feature-flag caveat.
Extend current Codex tests to assert installed assets and new capability states.

key_findings: Codex is already a first-class detected client in Fabric, but only for MCP config.
Capability reporting is now stale relative to official Codex docs.
Repo-scoped skill support means Fabric does not need a Codex-only private skill directory to add initialization workflows.
Repo-scoped hooks.json support means Fabric can build a Codex-native follow-up flow after fab init.
Because Codex tool hooks are still Bash-centric, SessionStart and Stop are the safer first integration points than PreToolUse/PostToolUse.

_metadata.exploration_scope: Codex support in init/config/bootstrap plus adjacent docs and prior analysis sessions
